Title: Different Chitin Synthase Genes Are Required for Various Developmental and Plant Infection Processes in the Rice Blast Fungus Magnaporthe oryzae

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class I chitin synthase; chitin synthase domain (IPR004835); multiple transmembrane domains
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major fungal cell-wall structural component required for hyphal growth, morphogenesis, conidiogenesis, and plant infection
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fungal cell wall of vegetative hyphae and conidia
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in conidia; low in vegetative hyphae; increased in appressoria and infected rice leaves; highly expressed in young developing conidia
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest in conidia and young developing conidia
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Tip region of hyphae and conidiophores; strongest at distal end of developing conidia; weak GFP in cytoplasm/vacuoles likely from internalized protein
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes proper chitin deposition during conidiogenesis and infection-related morphogenesis; loss severely reduces virulence
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Targeted gene deletion; qRT-PCR; conidiation and germination assays; Calcofluor white staining; SEM; appressorium and penetration assays; virulence assays on barley and rice; CHS1-eGFP complementation/localization
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHS1 deletion caused production of mostly single-celled, abnormal conidia lacking septa and spore tip mucilage. 2. The mutant showed reduced germination and appressorium formation and failed to develop invasive hyphae efficiently. 3. CHS1 is highly expressed in young conidia and its eGFP fusion complemented the mutant phenotypes.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native CHS1 promoter for CHS1-eGFP complementation
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Artificial hydrophobic surfaces; plant surfaces; rice leaf sheath penetration assays; barley and rice infection assays
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR expression profiling; no public omics accession reported
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion caused severe loss of virulence and conidiation defects
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although CHS1 encodes a catalytic chitin synthase, its experimentally emphasized role in this paper is a specialized morphogenetic/cell-wall function rather than nutrient quality metabolism. Chitin content in conidia increased ~30% in the mutant, suggesting compensatory activity by other CHS genes.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MMD-containing chitin synthase; class VI chitin synthase; N-terminal myosin motor domain; cytochrome b5-like domain; chitin synthase domain; transmembrane domains
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major fungal cell-wall structural component supporting polarized hyphal growth, penetration, and invasive growth
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fungal cell wall of vegetative hyphae and conidia
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in vegetative hyphae and infected rice leaves
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in vegetative hyphae and infected rice leaves
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential for pathogenicity; required for vegetative growth, conidiation, penetration, invasive growth, and normal conidial chitin level
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Targeted gene deletion; growth and stress assays; chitin content assay; microsomal chitin synthase activity assay; penetration assays; DAB ROS staining; wound inoculation; genetic cosegregation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHS6 deletion reduced growth and conidiation and rendered the fungus nonpathogenic. 2. The mutant formed appressoria but failed to penetrate host cells or form invasive hyphae. 3. CHS6 and CHS5 have overlapping roles, because the chs5 chs6 double mutant showed more severe growth and differentiation defects than chs6 alone.
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CHS5 (functional overlap); possible regulatory link with Mig1/Rlm1 ortholog discussed but not directly validated
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Osmotic stress, cell wall stress, Nikkomycin Z; onion epidermis and barley/rice infection assays
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR expression profiling; no public omics accession reported
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion increases stress sensitivity and abolishes pathogenicity
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Conidial chitin content was reduced ~40% in the mutant. Strong host ROS accumulation around attempted penetration suggests CHS6 is needed to overcome plant defense during penetration.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Chitin synthase; chitin synthase domain (IPR004835); multiple transmembrane domains
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major fungal cell-wall structural component involved in appressorium differentiation, penetration, and invasive growth
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest among CHS genes in conidia and appressoria; lowest in vegetative hyphae and infected leaves
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for germ tube-mediated appressorium formation on artificial hydrophobic surfaces and for full virulence, penetration, and invasive growth
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Targeted gene deletion; appressorium assays on artificial and plant surfaces; penetration assays; virulence assays; CHS7-eGFP complementation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHS7 deletion blocked appressorium formation by germ tubes on artificial hydrophobic surfaces but not on plant surfaces or by hyphal tips. 2. The mutant was reduced in virulence and defective in penetration and invasive growth. 3. Cutin monomer restored appressorium formation by mutant germ tubes on artificial surfaces, indicating surface- and cell-type-specific control.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native CHS7 promoter for CHS7-eGFP complementation
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Artificial hydrophobic surfaces; plant surfaces; cutin monomer 1,16-hexadecanediol treatment
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR expression profiling; no public omics accession reported
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion reduces infection efficiency and invasive growth
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper emphasizes CHS7 as having a unique surface- and cell-type-specific role in appressorium formation.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class I chitin synthase; glycosyltransferase family 2; chitin synthase domain; transmembrane domains
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Chitin biosynthesis / fungal cell wall polysaccharide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Cell wall chitin polymer synthesis during conidiogenesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Polymerizes N-acetylglucosamine from UDP-N-acetyl-D-glucosamine into chitin chains in the fungal cell wall
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Structural cell wall polymer required for fungal development and infection
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Cell wall of conidia and hyphae
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in conidia, especially young developing conidia
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Tip region of hyphae, conidiophores, and distal end of developing conidia
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes chitin-dependent conidium morphogenesis and virulence
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene knockout; chitin content assay; chitin synthase activity assay; complementation with CHS1-eGFP; localization; infection assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHS1 is a conidiogenesis-associated chitin synthase required for normal septation and spore tip mucilage. 2. Loss of CHS1 severely compromises germination, appressorium formation, penetration, and virulence. 3. CHS1 accumulates at growing tips and in young conidia.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Hydrophobic surfaces and plant infection conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antifungal target to impair conidiation and infection-related development in rice blast fungus; inhibition may reduce inoculum production and host penetration
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Deletion increased conidial chitin content, indicating network compensation by other CHS enzymes.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class VI chitin synthase; myosin motor domain-containing chitin synthase; cytochrome b5-like domain; chitin synthase domain; transmembrane domains
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Chitin biosynthesis / fungal cell wall polysaccharide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes chitin polymer from UDP-N-acetyl-D-glucosamine for fungal cell wall assembly during vegetative and invasive growth
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for pathogenic growth and penetration, based on mutant phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall polymer essential for hyphal integrity, penetration, and invasive growth
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Cell wall of vegetative hyphae, appressoria, and invasive hyphae
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in vegetative hyphae and infected rice leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in vegetative hyphae and infected rice leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential for pathogenicity and contributes strongly to chitin synthesis supporting polarized growth and host invasion
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene knockout; chitin content assay; enzyme activity assay; stress assays; penetration and wound inoculation assays; cosegregation analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHS6 is required for normal growth, conidiation, and cell wall stress tolerance. 2. The chs6 mutant forms appressoria but cannot penetrate or grow invasively in host cells, making it nonpathogenic. 3. CHS5 partially overlaps with CHS6, but CHS6 is the dominant determinant of pathogenic growth.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: NaCl, sorbitol, Calcofluor white, Congo red, Nikkomycin Z; host infection assays
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate fungicide target because loss eliminates pathogenicity; dual targeting with CHS5-related functions may further weaken polarized growth
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Conidial chitin content fell substantially in the mutant; mutant-triggered ROS suggests compromised cell wall/infection interface.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Chitin synthase; chitin synthase domain; transmembrane domains
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Chitin biosynthesis / fungal cell wall polysaccharide biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Polymerizes N-acetylglucosamine units from UDP-N-acetyl-D-glucosamine into chitin required for appressorium differentiation and infection structures
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Structural polymer supporting appressorium development and invasion
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Cell wall of germ tubes, appressoria, and invasive structures
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports germ tube-mediated appressorium formation on artificial surfaces and contributes to penetration/invasive growth
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene knockout; appressorium assays across surfaces; penetration assays; virulence assays; complementation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHS7 has a unique, surface- and cell-type-specific role in appressorium formation. 2. Deletion blocks appressorium formation by germ tubes on artificial surfaces but not on plant surfaces or by hyphal tips. 3. CHS7 is required for efficient penetration and invasive growth, thereby contributing to virulence.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Hydrophobic artificial surfaces; onion epidermis; rice leaf sheath; barley leaves; cutin monomer treatment
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antifungal target to disrupt appressorium-mediated host invasion, especially early surface sensing/differentiation steps
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Cutin monomer bypass of the appressorium defect suggests CHS7 acts in a surface-context-dependent infection program.
